mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks, providing a standardized approach for agent communication and management. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and potential learning curve for users unfamiliar with Kubernetes.

steps.title: Add the Kagenti Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Kagenti Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Kagenti using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Kagenti Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Kagenti CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required by Kagenti for managing its resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/crds/kagenti.io_kagent_crd.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRD for Kagenti.

steps.title: Install Kagenti using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti application in a specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --create-namespace --version 0.5.1

```

> Installs Kagenti in the 'kagenti' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource to demonstrate how to use Kagenti.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: kagenti/agent:latest
EOF

```

> This command creates a sample agent resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: kagenti/agent:latest
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the created Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kagents -n kagenti

```

> This command lists all KAgents in the 'kagenti' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the KAgent resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstalls the Kagenti application.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup any important data. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get kagents -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --version 0.5.1

kubectl get kagents -n kagenti

```

> Backup existing KAgent resources.
> Upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify that the KAgent resources are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kagents -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KAgent not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified KAgent resource does not exist in the namespace.

**Fix:**
Check the KAgent name and ensure it was created successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image for the KAgent is not available or the image name is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and ensure it is accessible from the Kubernetes cluster.

troubleshooting.title: KAgent is in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There are insufficient resources available in the cluster to schedule the KAgent pod.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits, and ensure there are enough resources available.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm release for Kagenti has not been installed or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Reinstall Kagenti using the Helm install command.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent types and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KAgent resource listed with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get kagents -n kagenti'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: kagenti/agent:latest
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources and tools installed.
